Your plan was attacked on four lenses — including BAR-LOWERING, the failure mode that caught every previous plan.

[
 {
  "refuted": true,
  "reasoning": "REFUTED under the BAR-LOWERING lens. Two independent problems: (0) the plan handed to me was empty — every field (PLAN DIAGNOSIS / RETRIEVAL / SYNTHESIS LANE / COMPOSER / EXPECTED) arrived as "undefined", so under "default refuted=true if uncertain" it fails on its face. I therefore refuted the only plan artifact that actually exists on disk, /home/polaris/polaris_project/sota_review/codex_plan_v3.md, against /home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md. It lowers the bar in five places, and I can quote each one.

1. THE SHIP GATE IS BODHI, VERBATIM. codex_plan_v3.md:368, "Rollout and measurement > Release threshold":
   "- Mean >=0.5441 across independent compositions."
   0.5441 is bodhi's task-72 overall (SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:18). The mission is >0.5578 (BRIEF line 10: "TARGET: > 0.5578 (cellcog-max, #1). NOT parity. NOT bodhi. THE TOP."). The plan's own stopping condition therefore fires 0.0137 BELOW the target, at exactly the number the brief names as the thing not to settle for. A compose that scores 0.5450 passes release, the wheel stops, and the mission is missed by design. This is the single load-bearing quote: the plan does not aim at 0.5578, it ships at bodhi.
   Worse: cellcog's task-72 score is UNPUBLISHED (only the corpus-wide mean 0.5578 exists; I confirmed /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/cellcog-max/race_result.txt contains only the 4 corpus-wide dimensions + Overall 0.5578, no per-task rows). Its task-72 score may well be above 0.5578. A gate at 0.5441 is not "close"; it is not even in the running.

2. THE EXPECTED OUTCOME IS CENTERED BELOW TARGET. Line 11: "My estimated outcome for the complete redesign is 0.53-0.56, centered near 0.55. Bodhi parity is credible; 0.5578 is an aggressive but defensible target." "Centered near 0.55" is a plan that expects to LOSE. And the ceiling table (382-383) makes it explicit: the full build — corpus + cards + synthesis contract + renderer — is priced at 0.53-0.56; the only row that clears 0.5578 is "Excellent execution with stable cleaner survival | 0.55-0.58", which attaches no mechanism, no lever, and no test. Beating #1 is left to a residual adjective.

3. EVERY QUANTITATIVE TARGET IS A CLONE SPEC OF cellcog, i.e. A TIE AT BEST. Lines 288-296 target the cleaned artifact at "12,500-14,500 body words" (cellcog: 13,580), "32-36 H3" (cellcog: 31), "median paragraph 70-95w" (cellcog: ~100w), "90-105 canonical journal sources" (cellcog: ~98). A perfect execution of this spec produces a cellcog look-alike, whose expected score is cellcog's score. There is no dimension on which the plan is engineered to EXCEED cellcog. BRIEF §6 (lines 125-131) specifies the out-scoring play — "cellcog's claims are ASSERTED. OURS CAN BE VERIFIED... 'Data and Factual Support'... a criterion we can OUT-SCORE cellcog on, not merely match" — and that play appears NOWHERE in the plan's five levers or in any rubric table. The plan's own "decisive mechanism" (line 387) is "verified journal evidence made visible in prose" — which is a parity mechanism (it gets us to what cellcog already does), not a surpass mechanism.

4. THE CORPUS GATE RETREATS BELOW cellcog TOO. Lever 1's release check (line 154) requires "At least 80 sources with usable method/result/limitation cards" and "at least six industries". cellcog has ~98 on-topic journal articles (BRIEF:108-119). The plan will pass its corpus gate 18 sources short of #1 while the brief says (line 96) "We have 17 (+32) = ~45. THAT IS NOT ENOUGH AND WE MUST NOT SETTLE FOR IT." Compounding it, line 114 rejects "reports, working papers, proceedings, books" wholesale — NBER/SSRN/IZA is where a large share of this literature lives, and cellcog itself pre-empts the working-paper objection in prose rather than excluding them (BRIEF:117). The plan's admission rule can only shrink the corpus, and it never states how many papers it expects to end with or how it knows it has enough — the COVERAGE MATRIX was supposed to be the stopping condition (BRIEF §8.1), and it appears in the diagram (line 46) but never gets a saturation criterion.

5. THE RETRIEVAL ROOT-FIX IS ABSENT. BRIEF §4 (lines 98-105) is unambiguous: the agentic retrieval loop (PG_AGENTIC_*, 15 rounds/120 queries, outline_agent) was never used, and "keyword search cannot find this literature" — Autor-Levy-Murnane (QJE 2003) contains neither "AI" nor "labor market" in its title; only CITATION-GRAPH EXPANSION finds it. codex_plan_v3.md's Lever 1 is a FILTER on the existing 105 rows ("Canonicalize and classify all 105 existing rows", line 347) — it audits what we already have. It never runs the agentic loop, never runs citation-graph expansion to saturation, and never adds a source. You cannot filter 45 on-topic papers into 98. The plan quietly redefines "retrieval at the root" as "corpus hygiene", which is the cheapest available reading of the hardest instruction.

CONFIRMING the lens's prediction: this is the same retreat every prior plan made. It is not a plan to beat 0.5578; it is a plan to reach bodhi, with a footnote that #1 "is aggressive but defensible."",
  "fix": "To be a #1 plan rather than a bodhi plan, the plan must change in five specific ways — each is a concrete edit to codex_plan_v3.md, not a tone change:

1. RESET THE RELEASE THRESHOLD ABOVE THE TARGET, NOT AT PARITY. Replace "Mean >=0.5441" (line 368) with: mean >= 0.5670 over >=3 independent composes x k=5 judges. Rationale: cellcog's task-72 score is unpublished; only its corpus-wide 0.5578 is known, so the true bar may be higher. The plan's own combined SE is ~0.010, so a gate at 0.5578 has ~50% chance of actually being at or below cellcog. Gate at target + 1 sigma. Delete "No composition below 0.52" — that floor is a parity artifact.

2. NAME THE SURPASS LEVER AND PRICE IT. Add a Lever 6: "Out-score cellcog on Data and Factual Support / Depth and Representativeness." cellcog asserts; POLARIS can carry a verifiable span behind every claim. Concretely: a per-claim evidence density target (e.g. every H3 carries >=2 named-journal, named-author, numeric findings with study design stated), plus the "Scope, Methods, and Source Selection" prose section that narrates our journal-only criteria AND our verifiability (cellcog narrates only its criteria). This is the only lever in the whole design that can produce a POSITIVE delta vs #1 rather than a zero one. Every other lever must be relabeled honestly as "close the gap", not "win".

3. TURN THE CLONE SPEC INTO A DOMINATE SPEC. Sources: >= 110 on-topic canonical journal articles (vs cellcog ~98), and the corpus gate must be a COVERAGE MATRIX saturation condition (every restructuring dimension x every industry the task names has >=2 independent primary studies), not a source count. Structure: match cellcog's shape (H3 count, ~100w paragraphs, 13k words, epistemic labels, structured abstract) — that is table stakes and should be stated as such — but exceed on evidence per section.

4. ACTUALLY DO RETRIEVAL AT THE ROOT. Lever 1 must ADD sources, not filter existing ones. Sequence: (a) seed with 10-15 canonical anchors (ALM 2003 QJE, Acemoglu-Restrepo, Autor, Frey-Osborne, Brynjolfsson-Li-Raymond, Felten-Raj-Seamans...); (b) run scripts/journal_corpus_build.py citation-graph expansion (forward+backward) to depth 2; (c) run the PG_AGENTIC_* loop (15 rounds / 120 queries) with journal-first query generation against the coverage matrix; (d) stop on matrix saturation, not on a picked number. Also drop the blanket working-paper rejection: admit high-citation NBER/SSRN/IZA papers as a separate, labeled tier and pre-empt the objection in the Scope section exactly as cellcog does — a wholesale reject shrinks us below #1 and buys nothing the rubric asks for ("high-quality, English-language journal articles" is graded by the judge reading prose, and cellcog wins that criterion while citing working papers).

5. ADD A KILL RULE THAT NAMES THE MISS. BRIEF §8.7 demanded an honest ceiling. Replace the "excellent execution -> 0.55-0.58" hand-wave with: "If, after full corpus saturation + dual-lane synthesis + cellcog-architecture compose, the k=5 mean is below 0.5578, the missing factor is X" — and force the plan to name X in advance (my candidate: the judge scores all 25 criteria in one comparative call, so cross-dimension bleed from readability is doing more work than any single lever, and the residual gap is likely instruction-following criteria we never read verbatim from criteria.jsonl). A plan with no named failure mode above parity is a plan that expects parity.

Until at least (1) and (2) are in the document, the plan should be sent back: its stopping condition is bodhi's score and it contains no mechanism whose success would put POLARIS above cellcog."
 },
 {
  "refuted": true,
  "reasoning": "REFUTED under the FABRICATION lens. A false claim reaches the page by two independent paths, one in the design and one in the code as shipped. I executed both.

PATH 1 — THE CORRECTED RULE ITSELF LICENSES FALSE CAUSAL ATTRIBUTION (design leak).
"A mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source" (SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:74) is not a faithfulness rule; it is a string-membership rule. /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py:198-205 already implements exactly this the moment the mechanism-bearing paper is one of the cited premises: `stated = {mm for p in prem for mm in p.mechanisms}`, then `any(mech in s.lower() ...)`. Nothing checks that the mechanism's SETTING (technology, era, level, population, outcome) matches the finding it is being used to explain. I ran the real validator with two real cards taken verbatim from /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/evidence_cards.json. It ADMITS:

  "The undetectable aggregate effect of generative AI is driven by the reinstatement effect, which is consistent with task creation offsetting displacement; the evidence does not establish a net decline."
  [premises: Acemoglu et al. (2022) JOLE null + Acemoglu & Restrepo (2019) JEP theory]

  "The muted aggregate response is attributable to information overload inside adopting firms, whereas the aggregate series does not establish an effect at the level of the economy."
  [premises: the 2022 AI null + Bresnahan et al. (2002) QJE, a paper about 1990s office IT]

Neither sentence is stated by any source. The 2022 paper reports a null and offers no cause. The 2019 paper is a theory of 20th-century robot automation and never claims the 2022 null is produced by reinstatement. Bresnahan 2002 never looked at AI. The composite proposition — "this specific empirical null is CAUSED BY that mechanism" — is a new empirical claim about the world that may simply be false (the null is more plausibly low adoption or a noisy exposure measure), and it is delivered to the judge wearing two prestigious journal names, i.e. it looks maximally grounded. That is precisely the red line the brief says must survive: "any attribution to someone who did not say it." The rule breaches its own line. Note also that bodhi's exemplar sentence HEDGES ("a pattern consistent with"); the corrected line drops the hedge, and CAUSAL_IMPORT (synthesis_contract.py:79-81) happily accepts bare "caused by / driven by / attributable to".

The mechanism pool is itself unverified. In /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py, only `claim` and `span` are span-checked against source text (line ~161); `mechanisms` is free-form LLM output copied straight into the card (line 167). A hallucinated mechanism string on any one card becomes a global license to assert that mechanism causally anywhere. Two-hop laundering: hallucinate a mechanism field, then "cite" its paper.

The gate is also trivially bypassable by paraphrase, so even the STRICT rule would leak. CAUSAL_IMPORT is a fixed trigger-word regex; it misses "accounts for", "operates through", "the channel is", "the mechanism at work is". The validator ADMITTED: "The mechanism at work is reinstatement: new task creation absorbs displaced workers, so the aggregate series does not establish a decline." And the new-entity check (rule 5) only inspects Capitalized tokens, so any lowercase fabricated entity passes untouched.

PATH 2 — THE CONTRACT IS NOT ON THE CRITICAL PATH (implementation leak). This is the fatal one.
`grep -rn "synthesis_contract" /home/polaris/wt/flywheel --include=*.py` returns exactly ONE hit outside the module itself: the import at cellcog_composer.py:49. `validate()` is imported and NEVER CALLED. The only thing standing between the composer LLM's prose and the page is `_clean()` (cellcog_composer.py:342-364), which strips `[n]` markers, meta-commentary and parenthetical years — no number check, no entity check, no mechanism check, no span check. The composer then prints "sentences DROPPED by the contract: N", naming a regex strip "the contract". So today, faithfulness is enforced by a prompt instruction ("you may NOT invent a causal explanation", line 296) — by asking the model nicely. "Inventing facts/numbers/attributions must remain impossible" is false as built: it is unpoliced. This is the same failure the repo already recorded twice (225b323 "mechanism never operated"; b3acb72 "the judge fell off the critical path").

PATH 3 — FABRICATION IS ALREADY ON THE PAGE, IN THE ABSTRACT. cellcog_composer.py:400-414 hard-codes the abstract. It asserts: "The review draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles identified through citation-graph expansion" — measurably FALSE (SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:85-92: 997 rows, top domains researchgate/bls/oecd/ssrn/nber, 120 journal articles of which 17 on-topic). It also hard-codes a Findings paragraph ("The evidence establishes displacement at the task and occupational level and productivity gains in controlled settings...") that is emitted verbatim no matter what the cards contain. These are unconditioned factual assertions in the position the judge reads most carefully, with zero gate between them and the page — and §5 of the brief proposes to EXPAND this into a whole "Scope, Methods, and Source Selection" section that "EXPLAINS ITS COMPLIANCE TO THE GRADER". If the corpus is not actually journal-only, that section is a fabricated compliance claim by construction, aimed directly at the instruction-following criterion.

By the lens's own stated criterion — "if it leaks, the plan is unshippable" — it leaks three ways. Unshippable as written.",
  "fix": "Three changes, all required before this ships:

1. TIGHTEN THE RULE FROM STRING-MEMBERSHIP TO SETTING-MATCH + HEDGE. A mechanism from source B may be attached to a finding from source A only if (a) B's declared fields (technology/era, level, population, outcome family) are COMPATIBLE with A's — enforce this as a typed check on declared card fields, not a substring search; (b) the sentence is rendered as a HEDGED consistency claim, never a causal assertion — allow "is consistent with <mechanism>, which <B-authors> describe in <journal>", forbid "driven by / caused by / attributable to / reflects / the mechanism at work is"; (c) BOTH sources are named in the running prose in the same sentence, so the mechanism is never asserted under the empirical author's name. Add a new operation MECHANISM_CONSISTENCY with these obligations rather than widening rule 6 of validate(). Add the three sentences I admitted above to ATTACKS as must-REJECT cases.

2. VERIFY THE MECHANISM FIELD. `mechanisms` must be span-checked against the source text exactly as `claim`/`span` are (cellcog_composer.py ~line 161), or dropped. An unverified LLM-extracted mechanism string must never become a citation license.

3. PUT THE GATE ON THE CRITICAL PATH. cellcog_composer.py must emit a structured sentence ledger (kind + premise_ids + operation) and run every sentence through validate() before assembly, deleting on failure — not import validate() and never call it. Delete the hard-coded abstract and Findings paragraph; derive them from admitted cards or omit them. Do not write any "we cite only journal articles" prose until a corpus audit proves it true; a false compliance claim is fabrication aimed at the grader."
 },
 {
  "refuted": true,
  "reasoning": "REFUTED — the plan is empty. Every field handed to me (PLAN DIAGNOSIS, RETRIEVAL, SYNTHESIS LANE, COMPOSER, EXPECTED) is the literal string "undefined". There is no lever, no mechanism, and no expected score delta to hold up against the JUDGE-INVISIBILITY lens, so the lens cannot be applied and the standing instruction ("Default refuted=true if uncertain") controls.

What I did verify, so the parent can re-dispatch against a real plan:

1. /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv — the GPT-5.5 board is 0-100 scaled (cellcog-max 55.78 top; sourcery 51.17; gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.98; grok-deeper-search 41.22 bottom). Columns are overall_score, comprehensiveness, insight, instruction_following, readability, citation_accuracy, effective_citations — and citation_accuracy/effective_citations are "-" for ALL ten entries. This directly corroborates the lens premise: because RACE strips [n] markers and the bibliography before judging, no citation-shaped work is scoreable in that call.

2. /home/polaris/polaris_project/benchmark_target.md lines 12-14 — RACE is a reference-based LLM judge over exactly four dimensions with per-task DYNAMIC weights: Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability, comparing the target report to a high-quality reference. Lines 25-26 further state that DeepTRACE (faithfulness) is a SEPARATE, complementary axis, not the mission's quality axis.

Consequence for the lens: for ANY candidate lever, the bar is that it must (a) survive marker/bibliography deletion — i.e. leave a trace in the prose the judge actually reads — and (b) push one of the four RACE criteria into the top band in a single side-by-side call against the 0.5102 reference. Levers whose entire mechanism lives in citation density, citation placement, bibliography quality, or retrieval provenance are invisible by construction and cannot move RACE at all. An unstated plan trivially fails both gates, since there is nothing for the judge to see.

I am not refuting a specific mechanism as unsound; I am refuting because there is no mechanism present to evaluate. Re-run this lens with the plan body populated.",
  "fix": "Re-dispatch this JUDGE-INVISIBILITY lens with the plan body actually populated (PLAN DIAGNOSIS / RETRIEVAL / SYNTHESIS LANE / COMPOSER / EXPECTED). The upstream orchestration step that interpolates those fields emitted the literal string "undefined" for all five, so the template variables were never bound — fix the caller's variable binding before spawning the lens subagents.

When re-run, the lens should test each lever against two gates: (1) does it leave a trace in the prose that survives deletion of all [n] markers and the bibliography, and (2) does that trace plausibly push Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, or Readability into the top band in one side-by-side call vs the 0.5102 reference (per /home/polaris/polaris_project/benchmark_target.md:12-14). Any lever whose value is carried solely by citations is dead on arrival — the leaderboard at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv shows citation_accuracy and effective_citations as "-" for every one of the ten entries."
 },
 {
  "refuted": true,
  "reasoning": "REFUTED — the "~100 canonical journal articles with usable full text" target is a wish, and the repo's own artifacts already contain the falsifying measurement.

1) THE CEILING WAS ALREADY HIT, AND IT IS ~10, NOT ~100. /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/journal_corpus_content.json (the best retrieval run to date: citation-graph expansion off 12 anchors -> 400 candidate DOIs -> Crossref filter -> Unpaywall fetch) holds 70 distinct journal articles with content_status: FULLTEXT 12 / ABSTRACT_ONLY 24 / CITATION_ONLY 34. So 49% is name-only. Worse, I read the 12 "FULLTEXT" rows: two are repository landing-page boilerplate, not papers — Frey & Osborne 2017 is 548 words starting "The future of employment... - ORA - Oxford University Research Archive CONTACT Name Email - Comment Send message FEEDBACK We welcome feedback about our service", and Tallon 2019 (JSIS) is 491 words of bepress chrome. Real usable full text = 10 papers, one of which is in a near-predatory venue (Int. J. of Advanced Trends in Computer Science and Engineering). Target 100, actual ~10.

2) CANONICITY AND OA FULL TEXT ARE ANTI-CORRELATED ON THIS TOPIC — the plan wants both and cannot have both. The papers we CAN quote are the OA long tail (AI and Ethics "opinion paper" on job-candidate reactions, PLOS ONE, Eurasian Business Review, Journal of Organization Design). The papers that MAKE it a canonical review — Autor/Levy/Murnane 2003 QJE, Autor 2015 JEP, Acemoglu & Restrepo 2018 AER / 2020 JPE, Goos/Manning/Salomons 2014 AER — are top-5 econ journals and paywalled. Live probe just now: Unpaywall on Autor-Levy-Murnane (10.1162/003355303322552801), which FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS itself calls "the most important paper in this literature", returns is_oa=FALSE with no OA location at all. Acemoglu & Restrepo 2018 AER returns is_oa=TRUE, but the "OA" URL is aeaweb.org's own PDF, which returns HTTP 403 Cloudflare "Just a moment..." from this box. is_oa is an upper bound, not text.

3) THE RETRIEVAL METHOD CANNOT REACH THE LITERATURE THE TASK IS ABOUT. Backward citation-graph expansion from 2013-2019 anchors is time-reversed by construction: the 70-paper corpus has ZERO papers from 2024/2025/2026 and only 3 from 2023. A 2026 review of AI restructuring the labor market with no generative-AI-era journal evidence is structurally stale. Forward traversal needs OpenAlex or Semantic Scholar; I tested both live: api.openalex.org -> HTTP 429 (still hard-limited on this IP, as the log said); api.semanticscholar.org graph lookup for DOI 10.1257/jep.33.2.3 -> HTTP 404 "Paper not found" (S2 not resolving from this box either). Crossref is the only API answering 200, and Crossref has no public cited-by traversal and — per the team's own measurement — returns ResNet/SMOTE sorted by citations and predatory title-matches sorted by relevance. There is no working forward-expansion tool on this box.

4) THE NEWEST POOL IS NOT PROGRESS, IT IS THE JUNK RE-ENTERING. corpus_journal_evidence.json (written 14:18, newest artifact) has 120 distinct DOIs — the 70 -> ~120 expansion the plan wants. Audited: only 51/120 titles are even loosely on-topic under a deliberately generous labor/AI/work/tech regex; tiers are T4=47, T6=14, T7=6, T5=3 (70/120 rows below T3). Venues include BMJ, CBE—Life Sciences Education, JMIR Mental Health, Youth & Society (1986), Journal of Technical Writing and Communication, Digital Education Review, International Journal of Multidisciplinary Futuristic Development, plus ImageNet/ILSVRC and the PRISMA statement. Same failure mode already documented in AGENTIC_OUTLINE_FINDINGS.md WEAKNESS 1 (a gap query fetched 104 off-topic medical papers). Intersecting on-topic AND T1/T2 AND span>=4000 chars yields 16 DOIs — and that 16 still contains "Transformation of Adolescent Peer Relations in the Social Media Context", "Delivering CBT to Young Adults", "Employee Volunteering" and PRISMA. The honest core is under ~10 papers: the same 10.

5) THE GATE MAKES THE SHORTFALL BIND HARDER ON POLARIS THAN ON RIVALS. cellcog's 98 sources are a citation count; POLARIS's strict entailment gate means a CITATION_ONLY paper may be NAMED but can never carry a claim, and the new typed synthesis contract needs premises that are themselves span-admitted. An ABSTRACT_ONLY row (~150-word Crossref abstract) cannot supply population/design/outcome/unit/horizon for a manifest, cannot support CONTRASTS_METHOD or RANK_EVIDENCE, and cannot be quoted. So the log's "36/70 usable" is inflated by counting abstracts as evidence; the substrate that can actually carry the deep-synthesis lane is ~10 papers / ~126k words, ~85% of it from 8 non-canonical OA articles. Any plan whose EXPECTED outcome depends on ~100 quotable canonical journal articles is planning against a corpus that does not exist and cannot be built with the tools that currently answer HTTP 200 on this machine.

Default-refute is not even needed: the plan's corpus target is contradicted by measurements the project already took, plus four live API probes.",
  "fix": "Do not plan against a 100-paper full-text corpus. Replace the corpus target with a measured, tiered contract and re-plan the synthesis lane around what actually exists.

A) TARGET = CONTRACT, NOT COUNT. Three tiers with different proof obligations (journal_corpus_fetch.py already computes them): QUOTABLE (>=1500 words of real article text; currently ~10) -> may carry claims and calc manifests; ABSTRACT_ONLY (~24) -> may carry ONLY a claim stated verbatim in the abstract; NAMED (~34) -> may appear in the literature narrative and the source-selection prose, never in a claim. Publish all three counts; never say "70 sources" or "36 usable" again.

B) FIX THE FULLTEXT DETECTOR FIRST — it is the 6th dead measurement path. journal_corpus_fetch.py accepted an Oxford ORA landing page (548w, "CONTACT Name Email... We welcome feedback") and a bepress page (491w) as FULLTEXT for two of the most canonical papers. Add a boilerplate/word-floor guard (>=1500 words AND no landing-page chrome AND title appears in body) and re-derive: FULLTEXT drops 12 -> 10 and the anchors correctly show as unquotable.

C) GET THE ECON CANON VIA THE WORKING-PAPER LANE, EXPLICITLY VERSIONED. The QJE/AER/JPE/JEP anchors have no reachable OA journal copy (ALM 2003 is_oa=false; AER 2018 "OA" URL is 403-Cloudflare), but nearly all have NBER/IZA/arXiv/RePEc/author PDFs. That is the only route to their text — and it is a faithfulness hazard on a task that says "only cite journal articles": cite the JOURNAL version in prose, quote the working-paper text, record version drift (numbers can differ between WP and published article), or forbid quoting numbers from WP copies and use them for mechanism/method spans only. Decide this deliberately; do not let it happen silently.

D) FORWARD EXPANSION NEEDS A TOOL THAT ANSWERS. OpenAlex 429s and S2 404s from this IP. Before any plan promises 2024-2026 coverage, prove one of: OpenAlex polite pool (mailto= + backoff, or a mirror/proxy), an S2 API key, or Crossref Metadata Plus cited-by. Until one returns 200 in a scripted loop, treat post-2023 coverage as UNAVAILABLE and disclose it in the report's methods section instead of padding with keyword junk.

E) KILL THE 120-DOI POOL AS AN EVIDENCE SOURCE. corpus_journal_evidence.json is ~58% off-topic and T4-or-below on 70/120 rows — the medical/education junk returning. Gate every new DOI on (venue is a journal) AND (domain-anchored relevance judge, not a regex) AND (tier <= T3) BEFORE it can enter the writer's menu.

F) RE-PLAN THE SYNTHESIS LANE FOR ~10 QUOTABLE + ~25 ABSTRACT PAPERS. That is a bodhi-shaped corpus (33 sources, 4.4k words, 0.5441), not a cellcog-shaped one (98 sources, 13.6k words). Chasing cellcog's shape on a 10-paper substrate is exactly how ranks 7-12 burned +5,800 words for a flat score. Optimize depth-per-source, in-prose journal attribution (format D — the only one that survives the cleaner), and an explicit source-selection-criteria section that tells the judge WHY the corpus is small and canonical."
 }
]

Write the FINAL SINGLE PLAN to BEAT 0.5578. Rebut or fix every refutation with evidence (quote the artifact or the
code). Do NOT retreat to parity. If after honest analysis the plan lands below 0.5578, say EXACTLY where it falls
short and what would be required to close it — but do not silently re-target.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

# BRIEF V4 — THE MISSION IS TO **BEAT** 0.5578. ONE SINGLE PLAN. UNLEASH EVERYTHING.

## 0. THE OPERATOR'S DIRECTION (this is the frame; do not narrow it)
> "Why stick with bodhi class? Why not the top one? ... If we have any old rules that hurt us from score, just
> drop it. If we don't have enough corpus to make us SOTA, then kick on the agentic outline search, or we do
> better at the early query generation + search + fetch + select + weight + dedup + consolidate, make them
> much much much better and richer so we are SOTA. Don't stuck with anything. I need one single solid plan,
> unleash the full power at the beginning, then keep the hamster wheel running till we match SOTA and beat SOTA."

**TARGET: > 0.5578 (cellcog-max, #1). NOT parity. NOT bodhi. THE TOP.**
Every previous plan (mine, Fable's, Sol's) quietly lowered the bar to match what had already been built.
**That is the failure mode to avoid. Design for #1.**

## 1. THE BOARD (task 72, our evaluator = GPT-5.5, from the benchmark's own per-task raw_results)
| system | OVERALL | compreh | INSIGHT | instr-follow | readability |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **cellcog-max** | **0.5578** (corpus-wide #1; no per-task breakdown published — its task-72 score may be HIGHER than bodhi's) | 0.5634 | 0.5708 | 0.5530 | 0.5194 |
| bodhi (best *known* task-72 score) | 0.5441 | 0.5441 | 0.5457 | 0.5559 | 0.5199 |
| lunon | 0.5406 | | 0.5594 | | |
| WhaleCloud | 0.5396 | | | | |
| gemini-2.5-pro-DR = **THE REFERENCE** (parity=0.50) | 0.5102 | 0.5111 | 0.5035 | 0.5187 | 0.5100 |
| openai-DR | 0.4743 | | | | |
| **POLARIS** | **0.4382** | 0.4549 | 0.4238 | 0.4409 | **0.3774** (worst on the entire board) |
Weights: **INSIGHT 0.32 | COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.29 | INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.25 | READABILITY 0.14.**
Measurement: judge SD **0.0074**; k=5 paired => **smallest resolvable effect +0.0094**. NEVER score n=1 again.

## 2. THE THREE CAUSES OF THE GAP (all measured, none disputed)
**A. THE JUDGE CANNOT SEE A SINGLE ONE OF OUR SOURCES.**
RACE runs an LLM cleaner that deletes every `[n]` marker and the entire reference list BEFORE judging.
MEASURED on 12 real facts x 5 citation formats through the PRODUCTION cleaner:
| format | authors survive | journals |
|---|---|---|
| `[1]` markers (**WHAT WE DO — all 240 of them**) | **0/12** | 0/12 |
| `(Acemoglu & Restrepo, 2019)` — the "obvious" fix | **0/12** | 0/12 |
| `Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019) show...` | 5/12 | 0/12 |
| **`Writing in the JEP in 2019, Acemoglu and Restrepo show...`** | **10/12** | **12/12** |
**It is the JOURNAL NAME that anchors the clause as prose. And the year MUST be prose — every one of
cellcog's 281 parenthetical `(YYYY)` is deleted.** What the judge reads from us today:
*"About 47 percent of total US employment is at risk of computerisation."* — a naked, unattributed
assertion. That is Frey & Osborne, 5,223 citations, peer-reviewed. **We did the scholarly work and hid
every trace of it.** On a task whose instruction is *"only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles."*

**B. WE CANNOT REASON ACROSS SOURCES — BY CONSTRUCTION.**
`entailment_judge.py:588` NEUTRAL clause deletes any sentence introducing *"a fact, entity, MECHANISM ... NOT
present in the SPAN"*. A cross-source inference IS that. **98% of our sentences are bare facts; bodhi's are ~45%
analysis; cellcog's synthesis prose is most of the document.** INSIGHT is 0.32 — the heaviest weight — and our worst.

**C. OUR REPORT IS PHYSICALLY UNREADABLE.**
12 paragraphs, **median 677 words**, ZERO H3. Every other system on the board — *including the two we beat* —
sits at 38-170 words/paragraph. **Our readability (0.3774) is the WORST SCORE ON THE BOARD.** And the judge scores
all ~25 criteria in ONE call, writing a comparative analysis BEFORE the numbers => **cross-dimension bleed is real:
our wall-of-text impression taxes the other 86% of the score.**

## 3. **THE FAITHFULNESS LINE — CORRECTED. THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT SECTION.**
I previously told the operator there was a hard trade-off: *"bodhi wins by writing sentences no single source
supports; we cannot match that without fabricating."* **THAT WAS WRONG.** Look at what bodhi actually writes:

> *"...aggregate impacts were too small to detect over the period studied **[3]** — a pattern consistent with GPT
> diffusion lags and within-firm reorganization preceding macro reallocation **[1]**."*

**IT CITES TWO SOURCES.** [3] is the empirical null. [1] is the GPT/productivity-paradox paper **WHICH STATES THE
DIFFUSION-LAG MECHANISM**. **bodhi is not inventing a mechanism. It is citing a THEORY paper for the mechanism and
an EMPIRICAL paper for the finding, and connecting them. THAT IS NOT HALLUCINATION — THAT IS WHAT A LITERATURE
REVIEW IS.**

Our gate kills that sentence for a stupid reason: it takes the UNION of the cited spans and asks *"is every
assertion literally present?"* The composite claim is verbatim in neither — **even though BOTH ITS COMPONENTS ARE
FULLY GROUNDED IN THE TWO PAPERS IT CITES.**

**THE CORRECTED LINE — design to this:**
- **FABRICATING A FACT** — a number, a study, a finding, an attribution that does not exist. **FRAUD. NEVER.**
- **CONNECTING GROUNDED CLAIMS** — a finding from paper A + a mechanism stated by paper B, with BOTH CITED.
  **SCHOLARSHIP. ALWAYS ALLOWED.**
**A mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source.** (The contract at
`scripts/synthesis_contract.py` currently requires the mechanism to be stated by the two papers being compared —
**too strict, it forbids exactly bodhi's winning move. FIX IT.**)
**STILL DELETED, DETERMINISTICALLY:** any number not in a source | any study/organisation that does not exist |
any attribution to someone who did not say it | any mechanism NO paper anywhere states | forecasts | universals.

**DROP ANY OTHER RULE THAT COSTS US SCORE AND BUYS US NOTHING.** The operator's instruction is explicit. Audit the
pipeline for rules that are pure cost: the 15-sources-per-section rule (unsatisfiable, silently padded), the
tier gate, the `[n]` marker convention itself (invisible to the judge — the bibliography can STAY for provenance,
but the PROSE must carry the attribution).

## 4. THE CORPUS IS THE OTHER HALF — AND OUR RETRIEVAL IS AIMED WRONG
MEASURED: `data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json` = **997 evidence rows, 919 distinct URLs, 530 domains,
206 DOIs, 107 journals — and EVERY row already carries a direct quote.** But:
- **only 5 rows had AUTHOR NAMES** (which is why in-prose attribution was impossible);
- after Crossref enrichment, **120 distinct journal articles — of which only 17 ARE ON-TOPIC.** The rest is
  **ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist, Cognitive Psychology 1974 (reading automaticity), Journal of Finance 1996.**
  **Prestigious, peer-reviewed, and completely irrelevant.**
- Top domains retrieved: researchgate.net (57), bls.gov (29), oecd.org (20), ssrn (12), nber (12) — **grey literature.**
**WE DID NOT HAVE A CORPUS PROBLEM. WE HAD AN AIMING PROBLEM.**

**cellcog has ~98 ON-TOPIC canonical journal articles. We have 17 (+32 I hand-built tonight by citation-graph
expansion) = ~45. THAT IS NOT ENOUGH AND WE MUST NOT SETTLE FOR IT.**

**WE HAVE AN AGENTIC RETRIEVAL LOOP WE NEVER USED FOR THIS.** (`PG_AGENTIC_*`: max 15 rounds, 120 queries,
convergence on theme saturation + URL overlap; `outline_agent`; the whole query-gen -> search -> fetch -> select ->
weight -> dedup -> consolidate chain.) **I spent hours doing MANUAL API pulls while the system's own search agent
sat idle. Fix the retrieval AT THE ROOT and run it to SATURATION against a COVERAGE MATRIX, not to a number I picked.**

PROVEN TONIGHT: **keyword search cannot find this literature.** Autor-Levy-Murnane (2003, QJE, 4,743 cites) is the
most important paper in the field and its title — *"The Skill Content of Recent Technological Change"* — contains
**neither "AI" nor "labor market"**. No keyword query finds it. **CITATION-GRAPH EXPANSION does.** Build that in.

## 5. WHAT #1 LOOKS LIKE (measured from cellcog's own task-72 artifact)
| | cellcog (#1) | POLARIS today |
|---|---|---|
| on-topic journal sources | ~98 | 22 |
| body words | 13,580 | 7,742 |
| structure | 9 H2 + **31 H3 + 8 H4** | 11 H2, **0 H3** |
| median paragraph | ~100w | **677w** |
| in-prose attributions | **133 author-year + 65 journal names** | **~0 visible** |
| epistemic labels | **14** (`[Established finding]`) — **THE ONLY SYSTEM ON THE BOARD THAT DOES THIS** | none |
| structured abstract | Objective / Methods / Findings / Contributions — **survives the cleaner in full** | none |
| **"Scope, Methods, and Source Selection" section** | **narrates its journal-only criteria IN PROSE — survives the cleaner 100% — it EXPLAINS ITS COMPLIANCE TO THE GRADER**, even pre-empting the working-paper objection | **we say nothing** |
| analysis vs reporting | ~half analysis | **2%** |
| `[n]` markers | 0 | 240 |
**"4IR" secretly means "COMPARE AI TO THE PREVIOUS THREE INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTIONS"** — ~11.5% of the score across
three criteria. We drop it after paragraph one.
**Our report OPENS with meta-commentary about itself** (*"This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence
on the question above"* — there is no question above) — **and the judge reads the opening and closing most carefully.**

## 6. **HOW WE BEAT cellcog, NOT JUST MATCH IT**
cellcog's claims are ASSERTED. **OURS CAN BE VERIFIED — every sentence traceable to a span in a real paper.**
The rubric explicitly grades **"Data and Factual Support: provides sufficient data, facts, cases, or evidence to
support its arguments and analysis"** (comprehensiveness) and **"Depth and Representativeness of Literature
Synthesized"**. **That is a criterion we can OUT-SCORE cellcog on, not merely match.**
**THE PLAY: cellcog's document architecture + cellcog's adjudication + DEEPER EVIDENCE THAN cellcog + grounding
cellcog does not have.** That is a #1 play. Copying bodhi never was.

## 7. WHAT IS ALREADY BUILT (reuse it; fix what is wrong)
- `scripts/corpus_audit.py` / `enrich_bibliography.py` / `corpus_enrich_merge.py` — Crossref enrichment (works).
- `scripts/journal_corpus_build.py` — citation-graph expansion from canonical anchors (works; **this is how you
  find the real literature**).
- `scripts/journal_corpus_fetch.py` + `deep_fetch.py` — Crossref abstracts + Unpaywall OA full text.
- `scripts/cleaner_survival_test.py` — **proved which citation format survives. Reuse the finding.**
- `scripts/synthesis_contract.py` — typed adjudication, 14/14, **ZERO false admissions. But its mechanism rule is
  TOO STRICT (see §3) — fix it to allow a mechanism stated by ANY cited source.**

Cheap test: select five apparent contradictions already in the bank. The system must produce a comparison grid and a bounded adjudication for each, with no new entity, number, or causal mechanism.

## Rollout and measurement

Do not spend a full compose until all mechanism tests pass.

1. **Corpus audit**
   - Canonicalize and classify all 105 existing rows.
   - This will reveal the real retrieval deficit.

2. **Cleaner-survival experiment**
   - Five cleaner runs per attribution form.
   - Then five paired RACE runs on the journal-only attribution retrofit.

3. **Synthesis-gate adversarial suite**
   - No full prose generation.
   - Zero unsupported admissions is a release requirement.

4. **Outline and two-module micro-compose**
   - One theory module and one cross-industry contradiction module.
   - Inspect the sentence ledger, not just the prose.

5. **Full compose**
   - Run three independent compositions.
   - Score each artifact five times.
   - With generation SD ≈0.016, three composes reduce generation SE to about 0.0092; five judges reduce the per-artifact judge SE to about 0.0033. Combined uncertainty is roughly 0.010, so a two-sigma detectable integrated effect is around 0.02.

6. **Release threshold**
   - Mean ≥0.5441 across independent compositions.
   - No composition below 0.52.
   - Zero faithfulness violations in sentence-ledger audit.
   - Cleaner-survival and corpus-admission checks remain hard gates.

## Expected ceiling

My non-additive estimate is:

| Configuration | Expected RACE |
|---|---:|
| Journal-safe narrative attribution on banked prose | 0.45–0.48 |
| Journal corpus repair + current fact-conveyor composer | 0.47–0.51 |
| New dual-lane composer without complete corpus repair | 0.50–0.53 |
| Full corpus + cards + synthesis contract + profile renderer | **0.53–0.56** |
| Excellent execution with stable cleaner survival | **0.55–0.58** |

The full redesign plausibly reaches bodhi and can reach cellcog, but 0.5578 is not guaranteed by matching paragraph length, heading count, or author density. The decisive mechanism is:

> **Verified journal evidence made visible in prose, organized into bounded cross-source adjudications that the current single-span gate cannot express.**

That is the path that preserves faithfulness while removing the actual reasoning bottleneck.===

# POLARIS BENCHMARK TARGET — the real scoreboard (from web search 2026-07-11)

## What we must beat
**FS-Researcher** (arXiv:2602.01566, ACL 2026) — "Test-Time Scaling for Long-Horizon Research
Tasks with File-System-Based Agents". A file-system dual-agent (Context Builder + Report Writer)
that scales research BEYOND the context window via a persistent knowledge base. This is the same
thesis as POLARIS (process ALL 329 baskets (current s2s3 iter-7 count; 346 was the stale iter-6 count), not one context window). It claims **SOTA report
quality** across backbones. Code: github.com/Ignoramus0817/FS-Researcher.

## The benchmarks it is scored on (both open-ended report quality)
1. **DeepResearch Bench** (arXiv:2506.11763) — 100 PhD-level research tasks.
   - Scorer: **RACE** — reference-based LLM judge, 4 dimensions with per-task DYNAMIC weights:
     Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability.
     Compares the target report against a high-quality REFERENCE report.
   - Download: `github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench` — tasks at
     `data/prompt_data/query.jsonl` (100 tasks); HF dataset `muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Dataset`.
   - Leaderboard (ChatGPT/Gemini deep-research RACE scores) is on HuggingFace.
2. **DeepConsult** — open-ended consulting-style report quality (pairwise/quality judge).
3. (also referenced for the agent side: BrowseComp, BFCL, TAU-Bench, MCP Agent Bench.)

Related: **DeepResearch Bench II** (arXiv:2601.08536) — rubric-from-expert-report diagnosis
(the DRB-II wrapper already stubbed in the compete wheel).

## What this means for POLARIS
- **RACE is the correct instrument** — confirms the compete wheel's direction; DeepTRACE (faithfulness)
  is a SEPARATE, complementary axis, not the mission's quality axis.
- "Beat FS-Researcher" is now RUNNABLE: run POLARIS on the DeepResearch Bench 100 tasks, score with
  RACE against the reference reports, compare to FS-Researcher + the ChatGPT/Gemini leaderboard.
- The two build wheels serve this end:
  - **OUTLINE** → depth + compute-and-prove drives Comprehensiveness + Insight (RACE dims 1-2) and
    the faithfulness moat (DeepTRACE) that frontier products lack.
  - **COMPOSE** → renders all 329 baskets (current s2s3 iter-7 count; 346 was the stale iter-6 count) fast + faithful so nothing is dropped for latency =
    Comprehensiveness + Readability, at full corpus depth.
- Open gap: we still have NO FS-Researcher report artifact locally; the benchmark download closes it
  (we can run POLARIS on the same 100 tasks and score head-to-head).

THE CORRECTED LINE, CORRECTED AGAIN — AND IT COSTS US NOTHING.

SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:74 says "A mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source." R2 is right that as a bare string-membership test this admits "The undetectable aggregate effect of generative AI is DRIVEN BY the reinstatement effect" with a 2019 robot-theory paper as the license — a new empirical claim about the world, delivered under two prestigious journal names. That is fabrication and it must not ship.

But look at what bodhi ACTUALLY wrote (SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:66-68): "...aggregate impacts were too small to detect over the period studied [3] — A PATTERN CONSISTENT WITH GPT diffusion lags and within-firm reorganization preceding macro reallocation [1]." bodhi HEDGES. It does not assert causation. So the hedge is free: it is exactly the sentence that beat us.

WHAT A SENTENCE MAY ASSERT (new operation MECHANISM_CONSISTENCY in scripts/synthesis_contract.py, replacing the widening of rule 6):
1. A finding from paper A, verbatim-grounded in A's admitted span, attributed to A in prose with journal + year.
2. A mechanism from paper B, verbatim-grounded in B's OWN span (QUOTABLE tier only), attributed to B IN THE SAME SENTENCE with journal + year, so the mechanism is never asserted under A's name.
3. The link between them ONLY as a hedged consistency relation: "a pattern consistent with", "which would be expected under", "the theory predicts". BANNED, deterministically: "driven by", "caused by", "attributable to", "reflects", "explains", "the mechanism at work is", "operates through", "accounts for", "the channel is". CAUSAL_IMPORT (synthesis_contract.py:79-81) is a fixed trigger-word regex today and misses half of these — it gets rewritten as a banned-verb list plus a dependency check for cross-premise causal predicates.
4. TYPED SETTING-MATCH, enforced on declared card fields, not substrings: B's mechanism may be attached to A's finding only if technology-era, level, and outcome-family are COMPATIBLE. This is what kills R2's admitted attack sentences: Bresnahan 2002 (era = pre-2010 office IT) cannot be attached to a 2022 generative-AI null (era = generative AI) — the typed check rejects it before any regex sees it. R2's three admitted sentences go into ATTACKS as must-REJECT cases; the suite is not green until they fail.

STILL DELETED, DETERMINISTICALLY: any number not in an admitted span; any study/organisation/person that does not exist; any attribution to someone who did not say it; any mechanism no paper states; forecasts; universals; and now, any causal predicate spanning two premises.

THE GATE IS ON THE CRITICAL PATH — THIS IS THE FIX THAT MATTERS. Verified: `validate()` is imported at cellcog_composer.py:49 and CALLED NOWHERE. The only filter is `_clean()` (defined :342, called :387) — a regex strip that the composer then PRINTS as "sentences DROPPED by the contract". Faithfulness today is enforced by asking the model nicely. Fix: the composer emits a STRUCTURED SENTENCE LEDGER (kind, premise_ids, operation, rendered text); every sentence goes through validate() before assembly; failures are DELETED and logged; the run aborts if >15% of sentences fail (that means the prompt is wrong, not the prose). The repo has already recorded this exact failure twice — commits 225b323 ("mechanism never operated") and b3acb72 ("the judge fell off the critical path"). Third time it gets a test: a CI assertion that a poisoned card produces zero poisoned sentences on the page.

THE HARD-CODED ABSTRACT DIES (cellcog_composer.py:400-414). input.the_wheel:
FIX -> COMPOSE -> SCORE k=5 -> READ THE PROSE LINE BY LINE -> FIX. Never n=1 (judge SD 0.0074; smallest resolvable paired effect +0.0094).

GATE 0 (blocking, before any build): retrieval preflight — 20 consecutive HTTP 200 forward-citation lookups, or forward expansion is declared dead and the kill rule arms.
GATE 1: coverage-matrix saturation (>=80% of 80 cells, no empty row/column, >=1 post-2022 study in >=6 industries, two consecutive agentic rounds adding <3 cells).
GATE 2: adversarial contract suite green INCLUDING R2's three admitted attack sentences as must-REJECT, plus a poisoned-card CI test proving no poisoned sentence reaches the page.
GATE 3: cleaner-survival — run the composed artifact through the PRODUCTION cleaner and count surviving author + journal mentions. Target >=120 journal names surviving (cellcog: 65 in-prose journal names + 133 author-year). If they do not survive, the lever did not happen.
GATE 4: micro-compose two modules (one 4IR-theory, one cross-industry contradiction) and READ THE SENTENCE LEDGER, not the prose. Every prior failure in this repo was a metric that moved while the mechanism never operated (commits 225b323, b3acb72, fb228dd).
GATE 5: 3 independent full composes x k=5 judges.

STOPPING CONDITION: the wheel stops when the BEST of the three composes has a k=5 mean >= 0.5670 AND the sentence-ledger audit shows zero faithfulness violations AND no dimension is below cellcog's published corpus-wide value (0.5634 / 0.5708 / 0.5530 / 0.5194). It does NOT stop at 0.5578 (coin flip against an unpublished per-task bar) and it categorically does not stop at 0.5441.
EACH TURN OF THE WHEEL: score per-dimension, subtract cellcog's per-dimension values, and spend the next turn's entire budget on the single largest deficit. No stacking of levers (fb228dd: stacked levers broke the length/density trade-off and the score went flat).

WHAT COULD STILL LEAVE US SHORT, IN ORDER OF PROBABILITY.

1. FORWARD CITATION EXPANSION STAYS DEAD (probability ~35%). OpenAlex 429s and S2 404s from this IP today. Without it we have no 2024-2026 journal evidence and no route to Brynjolfsson-Li-Raymond QJE 2025, Noy-Zhang Science 2023, or Eloundou et al. Science 2024. A 2026 review of AI and the labor market with no generative-AI-era journal evidence is structurally stale, and the #1 system leans on that evidence 164 times. Mitigations, in order: OpenAlex polite pool with mailto + 1 req/s backoff; an S2 API key (cheap, ask for it); Crossref Metadata Plus cited-by; a proxy/egress IP. If all four fail we cannot reach #1 on this box and the honest ceiling is ~0.55.

2. THE FULL-TEXT SUBSTRATE STAYS AT ~10 PAPERS (probability ~25%). Then mechanism spans, method contrasts and calc manifests are thin, and the insight lane runs on 10 papers' worth of mechanism. Mitigation is the working-paper text lane (NBER/IZA/arXiv PDFs of papers whose JOURNAL version we cite), which should take QUOTABLE from ~10 to 25-40. If PDF fetching is also blocked, insight caps around 0.52 and we land ~0.550.

3. CROSS-DIMENSION BLEED IS BIGGER THAN MODELLED (probability ~20%, and it cuts BOTH ways). The judge scores all ~25 criteria in ONE comparative call against the 0.5102 reference and writes the comparative analysis BEFORE the numbers. Our readability fix may therefore be worth more than the 0.021 I booked — or cellcog's polish may dominate ours in a way no lever touches.

4. cellcog's TASK-72 SCORE IS ABOVE 0.5578 (probability ~40%, unknowable). Confirmed: drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/cellcog-max/ publishes only corpus-wide dimensions, no per-task row. This is precisely why the release gate is 0.5670 and not 0.5578.

WHAT I AM NOT CLAIMING: that any single lever here has a measured effect size. Every number in the expected-total is an estimate from the board's own spread, and the repo's history (commits fb228dd, e87f52e, 225b323) is a graveyard of levers that moved a metric while the mechanism never operated. That is why every gate above tests the MECHANISM (does the journal name survive the cleaner? does the poisoned card get deleted? did the matrix cell fill?) and not the score.
input.kill_rule:
DECLARED IN ADVANCE, BEFORE THE FIRST COMPOSE.

IF the best of 3 composes has a k=5 mean BELOW 0.5578, we do NOT re-target, re-weight, or re-explain. We read the per-dimension deficits against cellcog (0.5634 comp / 0.5708 insight / 0.5530 IF / 0.5194 read) and declare which of these three named factors fired:

X1 — CORPUS RECENCY. Fires if forward expansion never returned 200 and the report carries <30 post-2022 journal studies. Diagnostic: our 2023-2025 mention count vs cellcog's 164. Required to close: a working OpenAlex/S2 egress path. This is the odds-on favourite and it is an infrastructure fix, not a writing fix.
X2 — INSIGHT SUBSTRATE. Fires if insight is the largest deficit while comprehensiveness and IF have closed. Means our cross-source adjudications are grounded but shallow, i.e. QUOTABLE tier stayed at ~10 papers. Required to close: the working-paper text lane at 25-40 papers, and mechanism cards with their own verified spans.
X3 — RUBRIC BLEED. Fires if all four dimensions land within 0.01 of each other and all are ~0.01-0.02 short. Means the judge's single comparative call is dominated by an overall-impression axis no individual criterion names, and marginal levers cannot move it. Required to close: prose quality at the sentence level (a human/LLM line edit pass on the full artifact), not more evidence.

HARD ABORTS, ANY TIME:
- If the sentence-ledger audit finds ONE fabricated number, entity, attribution, or unhedged cross-source causal claim on the page, the artifact is BURNED regardless of score. A 0.60 that fabricates is a 0.00.
